variant: build me a full skincare starter guide: a product table, a timeline, a checklist, and key stats

request.message: build me a full skincare starter guide: a product table, a timeline, a checklist, and key stats

response_body.response: here's what i've got for you — take a look:

products.name: CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser
products.description: Foaming yet non-stripping — for oily/acne-prone skin types.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Hero Cosmetics Mighty Patch Original (36ct)
products.description: Hydrocolloid patches — pull pus from active spots, prevent picking.

products.name: The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1%
products.description: 10% niacinamide for sebum control and pore appearance.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

visual_blocks.title: core skincare products for acne-prone combo skin

rows: preps skin for absorption, no irritation
targets acne, redness, pih without harsh irritation
pulls pus, prevents picking, speeds healing
prevents uv damage and pigmentation worsening

steps.label: weeks 1–2: barrier repair
steps.detail: cleanser + hydrating toner + lightweight moisturizer 2× daily. skip actives. goal: stabilize skin, reduce sensitivity.

steps.label: weeks 3–4: introduce azelaic acid
steps.detail: add azelaic acid 3× per week (mon/wed/fri evenings). use mighty patch on active spots. watch for irritation; dial back if needed.

steps.label: weeks 5–8: consistency phase
steps.detail: azelaic acid 5× per week. add a calming serum (centella, panthenol). spf daily without fail. acne should plateau or begin declining.

steps.label: weeks 9–12: refinement
steps.detail: azelaic acid nightly if tolerated. introduce a second mild active (niacinamide) if skin is stable. monitor for texture/pih improvement. expect 30–50% reduction in active breakouts by week 12.
